original_abstract: The proliferation of multisensor technology and the explosion of big data have exposed the inherent limitations of traditional flat-view matrix models, signaling a critical need for more versatile analytical frameworks. We demonstrate that higher-order tensors—or multiway arrays—facilitate a fundamental paradigm shift toward models that are essentially polynomial in nature. Crucially, unlike conventional matrix methods, the uniqueness of these tensor models is guaranteed under remarkably mild and natural conditions. Leveraging the mathematical rigor of multilinear algebra, tensor decompositions offer unparalleled flexibility in applying constraints that align with intrinsic data properties. This approach enables the extraction of more complex latent structures, far surpassing the capabilities of standard matrix-based techniques.
humanized_abstract: Multisensor systems and the deluge of big data have exposed the limitations of classical flat-view matrix based methods and models. Higher-order tensors or multiway arrays provide a new paradigm and new models that are inherently polynomial. In contrast to matrix methods, tensor models have a unique solution under very mild and therefore more than likely natural conditions. Exploiting the mathematical power of multilinear algebra, tensor decompositions provide possibilities to impose constraints that are more in line with the inherent properties of the data. In particular, these models are capable of capturing more complex hidden patterns in the data than any of their matrix based counterparts.

In particular, these models are capable of capturing more complex hidden patterns in the data than any of their matrix based counterparts.
